search_queries_used: site:bsaci.org rhinitis guideline nasal crusting examination septum polyps 2017
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic dry rhinitis, infection, vasculitis, drug injury, or septal-perforation diagnosis
automatic saline, ointment, antibiotic, steroid, imaging, biopsy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Nasal crusting is nonspecific and may reflect environmental, inflammatory, traumatic, infectious, systemic, or postoperative causes.
No investigation, medicine, treatment, procedure, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children or adults with nasal crusting in a possible rhinitis context; trauma, surgery, cocaine exposure, vasculitis, immunocompromise, and severe bleeding require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, allergy, rheumatology, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: BSACI guidance requires UAE environmental, occupational, rheumatology, infection, prescribing, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BSACI 2017 exact history, examination, and investigation sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 revised edition remains listed by BSACI; material recency uncertainty remains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact rhinitis history section explicitly supports crusting, discharge, obstruction, bleeding and contextual symptom documentation, including onset, impact, associated features, and relevant negatives, without assigning a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed general, nasal, septal, secretion, crusting, polyp, and throat assessment without auto-populating findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation context supports review of existing specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without recommending tests, topical products, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, dryness, bleeding, discharge, pain, obstruction, environmental and product exposure, trauma, surgery, systemic symptoms, examination, diagnosis, investigation, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
